title: glycine max subsp. soja
intro: Glycine max subsp. soja, commonly known as soybean, is a sub-species of the Glycine max plant, a legume native to East Asia, that is cultivated for its edible bean. The medicinal properties of Glycine max subsp. soja include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an anti-inflammatory. The health benefits of consuming Glycine max subsp. soja include reducing the risk of heart disease, certain cancers, and osteoporosis, as well as aiding in weight management and improving cognitive function. Glycine max subsp. soja can be prepared in various herbal forms, including dried leaves, tea, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and overall well-being.
